description_long: This bungalow features a long, rectangular footprint with a low-pitched, geometric roof that displays a striking pattern of parallel ridges and triangular facets. The roof overhangs the structure, providing shade and architectural interest, while the exterior walls are finished in a combination of dark and light materials, likely wood or composite siding with large glass window panels. The front facade is punctuated by a series of wide, horizontally-aligned windows, suggesting an emphasis on natural light and indoor-outdoor connectivity. The side elevations reveal a consistent window rhythm and a subtle variation in wall treatments, enhancing visual depth. The overall design is minimalist and modern, with clean lines and a strong horizontal emphasis. There is no visible porch, and the structure appears to be a single-story dwelling.
description: A modern, single-story bungalow with a geometric, patterned roof and large horizontal windows, finished in contrasting dark and light materials.
description_view_0: Front view showing wide horizontal windows and the full length of the geometric roof pattern.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the triangular roof facets and the narrow width of the structure.
description_view_2: Rear view with a similar window arrangement and roof pattern as the front, but with subtle wall treatment variations.
description_view_3: Opposite end view emphasizing the roof's triangular geometry and minimal window presence.
